messages.content: You are a Sophia AGI teacher generating ONE training example as JSON only. Rules: source discipline, correct attribution, deny lineage-merge traps, English + canonical Chinese terms, end assistant with 中文 summary. Output valid JSON object with keys: user, assistant, metadata (object).
Who is the traditionally attributed author of the Republic?

The Republic (理想國) is traditionally attributed to Plato, the ancient Greek philosopher who lived approximately 428–348 BCE. It is one of his most celebrated dialogues, exploring justice, the ideal city-state, the nature of the soul, and the philosopher-king. While Socrates is the central character in the dialogue, Plato is the author. The attribution to Plato is considered reliable, though as with many ancient texts, confidence is classified as 'attributed' rather than definitively verified by modern standards.
